concerns.text: Phase 7 tasks T065-T068 implement experimental validation protocol, experimental benchmark dataset loader, conformational ensemble sampler, and hydration state control experiment. These directly contradict spec.md Assumptions which explicitly state: 'Physical measurement validation is out of scope', 'Conformational ensembles: Single lowest-energy conformer per molecule from QM9 is used; ensemble sampling is documented as future work', and 'Hydration state limitation: QM9 molecules are gas-phase DFT calculations without explicit solvent; hydration effects are out-of-scope'. Tasks implementing out-of-scope work violate constitutional scope boundaries.

FR-010, SC-003, FR-013 require 6h/2CPU/8GB constraints to apply to 'entire pipeline from data download through final visualization'. T049 (time limit), T050 (CPU constraint), T052 (memory constraint) are in Phase 6 (Validation) which executes AFTER user story training tasks (T028-T029). This means constraints cannot be enforced during actual training runs, silently weakening the spec's requirement that constraints apply throughout execution.

User Story 1 acceptance scenario 3 requires: 'molecules are flagged and excluded with a report of excluded count (edge case acceptance criteria)'. T019 handles missing 3D coordinates but does not explicitly generate the exclusion report mentioned in the acceptance criteria. This drops an acceptance criterion from the spec.

SC-002 requires: 'Feature attribution analysis identifies at least 3 structural features contributing to predictive variance'. No task explicitly validates that the attribution output contains ≥3 ranked features before considering the task complete. This drops an acceptance criterion from the spec.

Phase 7 tasks are marked [ ] (unchecked) while all other tasks are [X], creating ambiguity about whether these are required for feature completion. If required, they contradict spec scope boundaries (see T065-T068 concern). If optional/future work, they should not be in tasks.md as implementation tasks — this should be documented in research.md per spec assumptions. This violates FR-030 Single Source of Truth principle.

FR-011 requires: 'System MUST validate predictions against QM9 quantum calculation reference data'. T032 references arXiv paper (2305.18454) rather than the QM9 dataset specification itself for validation. This is a semantic weakening of the validation requirement — the arXiv paper may discuss methodology but does not constitute the QM9 reference data ground truth specified in FR-011.
